frontmatter.title: 5 Must-Visit BTS Spots in Busan
frontmatter.description: From Jimin's childhood neighborhood to V's favorite beachside park, retrace the footsteps of icons in their hometown.

content:
Busan holds a special place in the hearts of ARMY worldwide. As the hometown of Jimin and Jungkook, the coastal city is scattered with childhood memories and iconic photoshoot locations. 

Here is our curated guide to the ultimate BTS pilgrimage in Busan.

<AdSlot />

## 1. Busan Citizens Park (Jimin's Spot)

In 2019, Jimin posted a photo of himself enjoying a quiet walk in Busan Citizens Park. The exact spot has since become a mandatory stop for any ARMY visiting the city. The park is expansive and beautiful, making it a perfect spot for an afternoon stroll.

**Photo Guide:**
Look for the trees near the South Gate. There are even footprints painted on the ground to mark the exact spot where Jimin stood!

<AffiliateButton href="#" label="Book Hotels near Busan Citizens Park" provider="agoda" />

## 2. Dadaepo Beach (Jimin's Vlog)

Another one of Jimin's favorite spots. He visited Dadaepo Beach and filmed a vlog here, showcasing the breathtaking sunset. It’s known as one of the best places in Busan to watch the sun go down.

<AdSlot />

## 3. Magnate Cafe

Owned by Jimin's father, Magnate is a spacious, industrial-chic cafe located in the Daeyeon-dong neighborhood. Inside, you can find a mini-museum of fan gifts, stunning art pieces, and even some of Jimin's stage outfits!

<AffiliateButton href="#" label="Get the Busan Tour Pass" provider="klook" />

Enjoy your trip to Busan, and don't forget to take plenty of photos!

frontmatter.title: The Magic of Dadaepo Sunset
frontmatter.description: Experience the dreamy golden hour where the city meets the sea, immortalized in many fan-favorite travel vlogs.

content:
Dadaepo Beach is famous for its vast sandy shores and spectacular sunset views. It's a place where you can quietly enjoy nature away from the bustling city center.

<AdSlot />

Many K-Pop stars have visited this beach to capture the golden hour. The reflection of the sky on the wet sand creates a mesmerizing mirror effect, perfect for taking silhouette photos.

## Best Time to Visit

Make sure to arrive at least an hour before sunset to fully enjoy the changing colors of the sky.

<AffiliateButton href="#" label="Explore Dadaepo Tours" provider="klook" />

frontmatter.title: Gamcheon Culture Village Vibes
frontmatter.description: Sip coffee with a panoramic view of the colorful hillside village and discover hidden street art.

content:
Often called the "Machu Picchu of Busan," Gamcheon Culture Village is a vibrant neighborhood filled with brightly painted houses, quirky art installations, and cozy cafes.

<AdSlot />

## The Little Prince Spot

You can't visit Gamcheon without taking a photo next to the Little Prince statue overlooking the village. It's one of the most iconic spots in Busan.

After exploring the narrow alleyways, relax in one of the hillside cafes and enjoy a panoramic view of the village cascading down to the sea.
